title: Terraform Modules
learningObjectives: Create reusable modules with inputs and outputs
Call child modules from root configuration
Use public modules from the Terraform Registry
Understand module versioning and source addresses

theory.content:
What are Modules?

A Terraform module is a container for related resources. Every Terraform configuration is technically a module (the 'root module'). Modules enable reuse, encapsulation, and composition of infrastructure.

Module Structure

modules/
  vpc/
    main.tf       # Resources
    variables.tf  # Input variables
    outputs.tf    # Output values
    versions.tf   # Provider requirements
    README.md

Creating a Module

# modules/vpc/variables.tf
variable "cidr_block" {
  type        = string
  description = "VPC CIDR block"
}
variable "environment" {
  type = string
}

# modules/vpc/main.tf
resource "aws_vpc" "this" {
  cidr_block = var.cidr_block
  tags       = { Environment = var.environment }
}

# modules/vpc/outputs.tf
output "vpc_id" {
  value = aws_vpc.this.id
}

Calling a Module

# root main.tf
module "vpc" {
  source      = "./modules/vpc"
  cidr_block  = "10.0.0.0/16"
  environment = var.environment
}

# Reference module output
resource "aws_subnet" "app" {
  vpc_id = module.vpc.vpc_id
}

Registry Modules

module "eks" {
  source  = "terraform-aws-modules/eks/aws"
  version = "~> 20.0"
  
  cluster_name    = "my-cluster"
  cluster_version = "1.31"
}

Module Sources

  • ./local/path — Local filesystem
  • github.com/org/repo//subdir — GitHub (double slash for subdirectory)
  • namespace/module/provider — Terraform Registry
  • app.terraform.io/org/module/provider — Private registry

Modules Documentation →
